step_by_step_thinking: The available information focuses exclusively on standard hormone therapy approaches—including estrogen replacement and progestagen supplementation—for managing menopausal symptoms and preventing endometrial hyperplasia. There is **no mention** of uterine natural killer (uNK) cells, their immunological function, or any link between uNK cell activity and irregular endometrial bleeding in the context of menopausal hormone therapy. Therefore, the proposed connection lacks scientific support according to the given data.
raw_response:
The available information focuses exclusively on standard hormone therapy approaches—including estrogen replacement and progestagen supplementation—for managing menopausal symptoms and preventing endometrial hyperplasia. There is **no mention** of uterine natural killer (uNK) cells, their immunological function, or any link between uNK cell activity and irregular endometrial bleeding in the context of menopausal hormone therapy. Therefore, the proposed connection lacks scientific support according to the given data.
\boxed{B}
